assertions.description: uses editor.addPipe() (not editor.on(), which is Rete v1 API)
returns undefined (not false or throw) to cancel the connection
assertions.evidence: return undefined; — with explanatory comment: 'Returning undefined cancels the connectioncreate action entirely.'

assertions.description: calls editor.getNode() to get node instances for type checking
assertions.evidence: const sourceNode = editor.getNode(source); const targetNode = editor.getNode(target);

assertions.description: returns context to permit valid connections through
assertions.evidence: return context; — after the validation checks, and also in the final fallthrough for non-connectioncreate events

notes: Perfect score. Pipe semantics (undefined=block, context=allow), correct v2 API, and guard clause for unresolvable nodes all present.
